title: Osiris Reading Glasses: Availability, Alternatives, and What to Know
description: Looking for Osiris reading glasses in Canada? Osiris is a UK Specsavers brand not sold here. Here are the best alternatives and where to find similar styles.
tldr: Osiris reading glasses are part of Specsavers' house brand range, available only in the UK, Australia, and parts of Europe. They're not sold in Canada. Similar reading glasses are available from Canadian optical stores and online retailers in the same style and price range.

tldrStats.label: Where Osiris reading glasses are sold
Reading glasses price range in Canada

definition.text: Osiris reading glasses are near-vision eyeglasses from the Osiris brand, a house label of Specsavers optical retail chain. They are available as both pre-made reading glasses and custom prescription reading glasses through Specsavers stores. Osiris does not distribute in Canada or the United States.

sections.content:
Osiris is a house brand of Specsavers, the UK-based optical retail chain. Their reading glasses come in two forms: over-the-counter readers at standard magnification powers, and custom prescription reading glasses fitted with your specific near-vision prescription.

Specsavers operates stores in the UK, Ireland, Australia, New Zealand, and parts of Europe — but not in Canada. So if you're searching for Osiris reading glasses in Edmonton or anywhere else in Canada, you won't find them through official retail channels.

Understanding the terminology helps you make informed decisions when shopping for glasses and communicating with your eye care provider. Many people feel overwhelmed by the technical language used in optical stores — but the concepts are actually straightforward once you know what each term means. Don't hesitate to ask your optician to explain anything you're unsure about. A good optician takes the time to educate rather than just sell.

At Charm Optical in Edmonton, the staff walks every patient through their options in plain language — no jargon, no pressure. Whether you're buying your first pair of glasses or upgrading to progressive lenses, they explain what each choice means for your vision and your budget. Book an eye exam to start with an up-to-date prescription, then take your time choosing frames and lenses with expert guidance.

sections.h2: Why Osiris Isn't Available in Canada
sections.content:
Osiris is a Specsavers-exclusive brand — designed and distributed only through Specsavers stores. Since Specsavers doesn't operate in Canada, there's no authorized distribution here. You might find the occasional pair on eBay or other resale sites, but secondhand reading glasses come with no warranty and may not match your actual reading prescription.

The good news: the reading glasses market in Canada is robust, and you have plenty of alternatives in the same style and quality range.

This is one of the most common questions that comes up during eye exams, and the answer often depends on individual factors that only a professional assessment can evaluate. What works for one person may not be ideal for another — your prescription, daily activities, facial anatomy, and budget all play a role in determining the best approach.

If you're in the Edmonton area, Charm Optical offers comprehensive eye exams that go beyond just measuring your prescription. Their optometrists assess your overall eye health, discuss your visual needs, and recommend lens and frame options tailored to your lifestyle. With direct insurance billing for most Alberta providers and same-day glasses for many prescriptions, getting the right eyewear doesn't have to be complicated or expensive. Book online — appointments are usually available within a few days.

Before shopping for alternatives, it helps to know which type you actually need.

Over-the-counter (OTC) readers are the drugstore glasses available in standard powers from +1.00 to +3.50. They're inexpensive ($10-30) and work fine for occasional reading if:

  • Both your eyes need the same magnification
  • You don't have astigmatism
  • You only use them occasionally (menus, labels, short reading sessions)

Prescription reading glasses are custom-made with your exact near-vision prescription. They're more expensive but offer:

  • Correction tailored to each eye individually
  • Astigmatism correction (which OTC readers can't provide)
  • More precise focal distance (optimized for your typical reading distance)
  • Better optical quality and coatings
  • Wider field of clear vision

If you're using reading glasses for more than occasional use, prescription readers are worth the investment. They reduce eye strain and headaches that OTC readers often cause — especially if your eyes have different prescriptions or any astigmatism.

Start with an eye exam to find out exactly what your near vision needs. Your optometrist will measure your reading add power and check for astigmatism.

sections.h2: Best Alternatives to Osiris Reading Glasses in Canada
sections.content:
If you like the Osiris look — clean, modern, affordable — these options are readily available in Canada:

For prescription reading glasses:

  • Calvin Klein: Clean, professional frames that work well as dedicated readers. Available at most Canadian optical stores.
  • Nine West: Fashion-forward women's frames at mid-range prices.
  • Polo Ralph Lauren: Classic styling, good for readers you'll wear in professional settings.
  • Ray-Ban: Recognizable styles that look as good as reading glasses as they do as everyday frames.

For over-the-counter readers:

  • Drugstore brands (Shoppers Drug Mart, London Drugs) carry basic readers from $15-30
  • Foster Grant and other dedicated reader brands offer fashion-forward OTC options
  • Online retailers like Amazon have massive OTC reader selections

For prescription reading glasses with professional fitting, visit Charm Optical in South Edmonton. The team can help you choose frames specifically suited to reading use — lighter weight, comfortable nose pads, and the right lens size for your reading habits.

sections.h2: Progressive Lenses vs. Dedicated Reading Glasses
sections.content:
If you already wear distance glasses, you have a choice: get separate reading glasses, or switch to progressive lenses that handle both distance and near vision in one pair.

Dedicated reading glasses make sense if:

  • You only need glasses for reading (your distance vision is fine without correction)
  • You want maximum reading field width (single vision readers give you the whole lens for reading)
  • Budget is a priority — single vision readers are much cheaper than progressives

Progressive lenses make sense if:

  • You already wear distance glasses and are tired of switching between two pairs
  • You need to see your computer screen clearly as well as up-close reading material
  • You want one pair that does everything

Your optometrist at Charm Optical can help you decide based on your prescription and daily activities. Both options are covered by most Alberta insurance plans.

Whether you go OTC or prescription, here are a few practical tips:

  • Get the right power. Too strong is just as bad as too weak. OTC readers should let you read comfortably at your normal reading distance (about 14-16 inches). If you need to hold material closer or further, try a different power.
  • Consider your reading distance. Music readers, computer users, and book readers all work at different distances. Standard reading glasses are set for about 16 inches — if your primary use is different, mention it to your optometrist.
  • Lighter frames are better. Reading glasses tend to go on and off frequently throughout the day. Lightweight frames are less annoying to handle and less likely to leave pressure marks on your nose.
  • Consider a neck chain or case. Reading glasses that live on your head or get tossed into bags get scratched and broken fast. A dedicated case extends their life significantly.

Book an appointment at Charm Optical to get your near vision measured and explore reading glasses options — from simple single vision readers to progressive lenses that handle all distances.

faqs.q: Can I buy Osiris reading glasses in Canada?
faqs.a: No. Osiris is a Specsavers-exclusive brand, and Specsavers doesn't operate in Canada. Similar reading glasses styles are available from Canadian optical stores and online retailers.

faqs.q: Are drugstore reading glasses bad for your eyes?
faqs.a: They won't damage your eyes, but they're not ideal for extended use. OTC readers use the same magnification in both lenses and can't correct astigmatism. If your eyes have different prescriptions or any astigmatism, OTC readers can cause eye strain and headaches. Prescription reading glasses from Charm Optical are fitted to your exact needs.

faqs.q: How do I know what reading glasses strength I need?
faqs.a: The most accurate way is an eye exam — your optometrist measures your near addition power precisely. As a rough guide, most people need +1.00 to +1.50 in their early 40s, +1.50 to +2.00 in their late 40s-50s, and +2.00 to +2.50 by their 60s. But individual variation is significant.

faqs.q: What's the difference between Osiris and similar Canadian brands?
faqs.a: Osiris is a mid-range house brand comparable to Calvin Klein, Nine West, and similar labels available at Canadian optical stores. The quality and price range are similar. Visit Charm Optical in South Edmonton to try on comparable frames in person.
